notes: This manifest tracks approved-host helper evidence for TUN runtime work. completed_on_approved_vm means the evidence slice exists; it does not mean product TUN packet I/O is ready. It does not authorize local-machine network changes, default-route mutation, global DNS mutation, or a new approved-host mutation run.

blocker_slices.notes: Phase A evidence exists for temporary TUN, documentation-prefix route, namespace-scoped DNS smoke, rollback, retained-journal recovery, and default-route/global-DNS unchanged checks.

Phase B namespace runtime smoke passed on approved-linux-vm with temporary network namespace, veth data path, namespace-local TUN, policy route, namespace-scoped DNS, leak sentries, rollback, residue checks, and unchanged host default-route/global-DNS baselines.

Production include/exclude route policy now has core production-policy gate coverage plus approved-VM namespace evidence for a preserved control-plane route coexisting with a default TUN route. Host-level route mutation remains disallowed by this registry.

Default-route policy now has core gate coverage plus approved-VM namespace evidence that default route selection can be pointed at a namespace-local TUN device and rolled back without changing the host default route.

DNS policy now has core gate coverage plus approved-VM namespace evidence that namespace-scoped resolver configuration and DNS route selection can use the TUN path while the host global DNS resolver remains unchanged.

Service-manager integration now has approved-VM transient systemd evidence for privileged helper lifecycle success, failure cleanup, rollback/residue checks, and unchanged host default-route/global-DNS baselines. No permanent unit is installed by the smoke.

Full privileged helper runtime integration now has approved-VM aggregate evidence for the current prototype scope: namespace runtime, default-route/DNS policy, service-manager lifecycle, leak/coexistence, rollback, residue checks, and unchanged host baselines. This does not claim production-grade full-device TCP/IP relay behavior.

Leak and coexistence testing now has approved-VM namespace evidence for default and DNS probes selecting the TUN path, preserved control-plane routing over veth, TCP echo coexistence, unchanged host listener/default-route/global-DNS baselines, rollback, and residue checks.
